title: Why wear plug suits And whats that on their heads
display_title: Why wear plug suits And whats that on their heads
wikitext:
[]: Why wear plug suits? And what's that on their heads?             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Wed Jul 19, 2006 8:19 am Well all Eva pilots wear plug suits in their Evas. They make them look cool it's all right, but there're occasions that the pilot can control the Eva as finely as they can even without their plug suits, like when Shinji confronted the 3rd and 14th Angel. So why would they need to wear those plastic things? And what's that thing they wear on their head? You always see Asuka has it on her head and it sounds to me it's nothing more, nothing less a piece of decoration.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Wed Jul 19, 2006 8:24 am Two reasons are obvious: Adam/Eve-dress is forbidden, since this is not a hentai. Secondly, it is a protective suit in my estiomation. And perhaps it boosts the synch. ratio a bit... I'm not too sure of that...

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Jul 19, 2006 8:33 am Sounds about right. It's recommended, but not entirely necessary. Oh, and Asuka wears hers casually to show off.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Wed Jul 19, 2006 8:42 am IIRC, they boost the sync ratio.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Wed Jul 19, 2006 8:44 am Imagine Asuka or Rei going into an entry plug full of LIQUID with school SKIRTS. That would make their clothes see-through. Plus, I think it would help them with manuverablity in high-sync rates. Flapping clothes would kind of interefere, so skin tight clothes allow for completely feeling the same as the EVA. Those things on their heads boost sync-ratio, I think. Allow for better connection between the EVA and the pilot's nerves. Asuka wears them for decoration, methinks, and to let everyone now that she's an EVA pilot so they can bow down and kiss her feet.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Wed Jul 19, 2006 8:52 am IIRC=? Imagine Asuka or Rei going into an entry plug full of LIQUID with school SKIRTS. That would make their clothes see-through.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Wed Jul 19, 2006 9:11 am So what's that piece of head decoration technically called?

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Wed Jul 19, 2006 9:16 am tinmeigut wrote: So what's that piece of head decoration technically called?

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Wed Jul 19, 2006 10:08 am Ask one of the Forum Elders. They tend to know many things about tiny details like this. I personally recommend Mr.Tines. He usually knows everything, especially the technical parts as I recall.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 19, 2006 10:51 am The thing they hear on their heads is called the interface headset in Japanese materials. As far as I know, "A^10... whatever" is just something ADV or another unofficial source made up. When not being worn, they look kinda like this: Granted that's not a very good picture... Production illos have a better one, but I've been too lazy to actually scan it. Basically the two "clips" are attached via that thing that wraps around the head beneath the hair. Unless you're Asuka and you must be special.

[]:             Quote Post by badc0de » Wed Jul 19, 2006 11:34 am That image looks somehow familar to me :shock: Reichu wrote: Basically the two "clips" are attached via that thing that wraps around the head beneath the hair. Unless you're Asuka and you must be special.

[]:             Quote Post by System Shocker » Wed Jul 19, 2006 12:54 pm The first time Shinji ever gets in an Evangelion, he is wearing his normal clothes

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 19, 2006 2:20 pm Reichu wrote: The thing they hear on their heads is called the interface headset in Japanese materials. As far as I know, "A^10... whatever"

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Wed Jul 19, 2006 2:24 pm System Shocker wrote: The first time Shinji ever gets in an Evangelion, he is wearing his normal clothes

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jul 19, 2006 2:50 pm There's also some extra stuff on the suit: ep16 wrote: IBUKI: The plug suit's life support system will be getting close to redline. --- Shinji: The heating and the oxygen circulation are already out. I'm cold. It's useless, the suit is giving out too.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Jul 19, 2006 3:51 pm Sounds just like the sneaking suit.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei_Unit00 » Thu Jul 20, 2006 10:33 pm It's for sync ratio, and I believe it also has life support functions, IIRC.
